name: Rough Draft Club
description: Weekly WIP demos with honest roasts. Ship ugly, get better fast.
vibe: Chaotic-good energy. We celebrate broken demos and fix them together.

messages.text: ok who's going first tonight. i have a landing page that's aggressively beige
me. fair warning it's held together with console.log and prayer
that's the spirit. beige is a crime tho mia
streaming my demo in 10 if anyone wants to roast live
the hero copy says 'revolutionary' unironically. i need help
delete the word revolutionary. all of it. start over
agree. also your CTA is the same color as the background. intentional?
...no. fixing now. this is why i come here
chat just said my font looks like a ransom note. they're not wrong
reminder: next week theme is 'worst first version'. prize for most embarrassing bug
i'm bringing redlines. bring tissues

summary: A weekly roast-and-ship crew obsessed with ugly first versions. Members share broken demos, get blunt design and copy feedback, and compete for the most embarrassing bug. Tone is playful but direct — no one leaves without actionable notes.

name: Night Shift Hackers
description: After-midnight builders who commit when the world sleeps.
vibe: Quiet until 11pm, then the channel explodes with commits and lo-fi links.

messages.text: 2:14am. just got auth working. might sleep might not
still up. push it. we'll review at lunch
chat is dead but i'm coding. anyone want a sync at 3?
3 works. sharing screen. warning: variable names are all 'temp'
temp2 is my favorite anti-pattern. see you at 3
shipped the extension. 47 tabs open. no regrets
VOD is up if you missed the 3am chaos
tonight: theme is 'one feature in one sitting'. no yak shaving
lo-fi playlist dropped in pins. see you after dark

summary: Late-night solo builders who sync after midnight for quick reviews and accountability. The vibe is low-key during the day, then bursts into commits, screen shares, and lo-fi playlists. Perfect for people who ship best when everyone else is asleep.

name: Feedback Loop SF
description: Structured peer critique for builders who want clarity, not compliments.
vibe: Timed critique rounds. You leave with three notes, not twenty maybes.

messages.text: Tuesday session: 6pm PT. Format is 10min present, 15min critique, no interruptions
signing up. bringing the onboarding flow that confused three testers
i'll go second. need help with positioning not pixels
can a first-timer lurk? want to see how harsh y'all actually are
lurking fine. we critique the work not the person. you'll get a turn when ready
notes from last week: 'your value prop is three value props'. still stings. still true
rewrote the headline. down from 47 words to 12. feedback loop works
rule reminder: one piece of praise max. we're here to improve
ok i'm in for next week. nervous but the luca method seems fair
whoever said 'your CTA sounds like a threat' — thank you. fixed it

summary: A structured critique group running timed feedback rounds for builders and writers. Sessions are firm but fair — one compliment max, three actionable notes guaranteed. Members share positioning, UX, and copy work; the tone is direct without being cruel.
